stem: A social worker meets with a client who reports hearing commands to harm a coworker. What should the social worker do FIRST?

options.text: Explore the client's childhood trauma history
Assess whether the client has a specific plan and means
Refer the client for long-term psychotherapy
Encourage journaling to reduce distress

rationale.correct: Safety and risk assessment come before exploratory work, coping strategies, or long-term referral.

incorrect.A: Trauma history may matter later, but not before danger is assessed.
incorrect.C: Referral is premature before the urgency and immediacy of the threat are known.
incorrect.D: This may be supportive later, but it is insufficient when there may be risk to others.

stem: A client who was recently hospitalized for suicidal ideation now states, 'I finally feel calm. Everyone will be better off soon.' Which concern should the social worker consider MOST important?

options.text: The client may be ready for discharge planning
The client may be minimizing ongoing suicide risk
The client is showing improved coping skills
The client is demonstrating acceptance of treatment

rationale.correct: Sudden calm in the context of recent suicidal ideation can signal elevated risk rather than improvement.

incorrect.A: Discharge planning is premature when risk may still be active.
incorrect.C: The stem does not support a conclusion of improved coping.
incorrect.D: There is no indication that the client is accepting treatment.

stem: For the past seven months, a client has experienced excessive worry, restlessness, irritability, and difficulty sleeping. There is no single precipitating event. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

options.text: Generalized anxiety disorder
Adjustment disorder with anxiety
Panic disorder
Acute stress disorder

rationale.correct: The duration and symptom pattern best fit generalized anxiety disorder.

incorrect.B: Adjustment disorder requires an identifiable stressor and does not fit the duration/pattern as well.
incorrect.C: Panic disorder centers on recurrent unexpected panic attacks, which are not described.
incorrect.D: Acute stress disorder is trauma-linked and limited in duration.

stem: During an initial session, an adult client who was sexually abused as a child becomes fearful and hesitant when asked for specific details about the abuse. What should the social worker do FIRST?

options.text: Change the topic to avoid retraumatization
Confront the client about avoidance
Acknowledge the client's reluctance to discuss the experience
Ask whether the client wants to terminate services

rationale.correct: Acknowledging reluctance is validating, trauma-informed, and appropriately paced.

incorrect.A: Avoidance of all discussion is not the best first move if the client is still engaged.
incorrect.B: Confrontation is likely too aggressive and not trauma-informed here.
incorrect.D: Termination is premature and not the first response.

stem: Which item is BEST understood as part of a biopsychosocial assessment?

options.text: Only the client's DSM diagnosis
Biological, psychological, and social functioning relevant to the presenting problem
A list of referrals made after discharge
Only the client's family history

rationale.correct: A biopsychosocial assessment integrates multiple dimensions of functioning relevant to the client.

incorrect.A: Diagnosis may be part of assessment but does not equal the full biopsychosocial picture.
incorrect.C: Referrals may follow assessment but are not the assessment itself.
incorrect.D: Family history may contribute, but it is only one part.

stem: A client with a history of hostility toward agency staff arrives for an intake interview and answers in one-word responses. What should the social worker do FIRST to begin building a therapeutic alliance?

options.text: Review all prior services the client has received
Engage the client in discussing the presenting problem
Explain that hostile behavior will not be tolerated
Refer the client to an anger management group

rationale.correct: Engagement around the client's current concern is the strongest first move toward alliance-building.

incorrect.A: This may matter later, but it does not directly build initial engagement.
incorrect.C: Limit setting may become relevant, but it is not the best first alliance move in the stem.
incorrect.D: Referral is premature before engagement and assessment.

stem: A social worker receives a call from a client's spouse asking whether the client is currently in session. The client has not signed a release of information. What should the social worker do FIRST?

options.text: Confirm the client is in the office but give no further details
Explain the limits of confidentiality in a general way
Ask the spouse to sign a release of information
Hang up immediately

rationale.correct: The social worker should protect confidentiality and respond in a general, nonconfirming way.

incorrect.A: Even confirming the client is present discloses protected information.
incorrect.C: The spouse cannot authorize release of the client's confidential information.
incorrect.D: Abruptly hanging up is not the best first professional response.
